mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold crossing fails to predict subsequent drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 240, rising federal funds rates fail to stimulate equity valuations because real discount rates remain elevated, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-CPI periods do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew relative to low-CPI periods, or FEDFUNDS changes show no predictive power when CPIAUCSL > 240.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands corporate profit margins and earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDP acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payrolls drop below 142000, it signals a sharp deceleration in economic activity that triggers risk-off positioning and negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Payrolls dropping below the threshold fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stagnant industrial output indicate debt-fueled consumption masking underlying production weakness, which leads to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and flat INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions where industrial production falls while housing starts remain elevated, inventory drawdowns compress corporate margins and drive negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns in supply shock periods do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew relative to normal periods, or INDPRO declines fail to predict subsequent drawdowns when HOUST is elevated.
